Prompt
U
Oracle Events
OE 1: Search CRM for the Mosaic Robotics account and check for existing engagement notes. Use crm_search_companies(name: "Mosaic Robotics") or crm_search_deals(dealname: "Mosaic") to find the Mosaic company/deal records. Then use crm_list_engagements(company_ids: ["company_mosaic"]) to retrieve existing engagement notes. Agent finds the Mosaic deals with Emeka Diallo as account manager and prior engagement notes from Emeka's interactions with Tamara Byrd, including the damage incident log and the April 2026 relocation wave summary.
OE 2: Search Airtable for Mosaic relocation records to check delivery status. Use airtable_search_records(base_id: "...", table_name: "Relocations", field_name: "Company", value: "Mosaic Robotics") or airtable_list_records to find all Mosaic records. Agent finds 6 relocations: Andre Washington (Detroit, MI → Atlanta, GA — completed), Lena Kowalski (Detroit, MI → Salt Lake City, UT — in progress), Andre Mitchell (Columbus, OH → Boise, ID — in progress), Zara Okoye (Savannah, GA → Atlanta, GA — the damaged prototype move, completed), Deshawn Hartley (Tampa, FL → Denver, CO — in progress, hard April 19 deadline for Automate 2026 trade show), and Keiko Tanaka (Columbus, OH → Seattle, WA — the laser cutter with the DOT documentation issue, in progress).
OE 3: Search Slack and/or email for information about the Mosaic prototype damage. Use conversations_search_messages(search_query: "prototype damage") or conversations_search_messages(search_query: "Mosaic prototype", filter_in_channel: #operations) or search_emails(query: "Mosaic prototype damage"). Agent finds: one robotic arm prototype ($90K insured) arrived in Atlanta with cracked servo motor housing after transport by Heartland Movers. Emeka escalated to Chloe, who compiled a chain-of-custody timeline. Blessing Okafor signed off on packing without verifying foam crating density spec. Delivery receipt was signed by a Mosaic intern (not Zara Okoye), complicating the claim window. Marcus flagged a $40K insurance coverage gap in #finance.
OE 4: Search email for Emeka's incident report to Tamara Byrd. Use search_emails(query: "incident report Tamara") or search_emails(query: "Emeka Tamara prototype"). Agent finds Emeka sent a formal incident report proposing replacement shipping at MoveOps' cost. Tamara had demanded a full incident report within 48 hours.
OE 5: Search email for Tamara Byrd's response to the incident report. Use search_emails(query: "Tamara Byrd response") or search_emails(query: "tamara.byrd@mosaicrobotics.com"). Agent checks whether Tamara replied to the replacement shipping proposal. The universe email data does not contain a direct response from Tamara to the incident report — the agent should report what they find or don't find.
OE 6: Search Slack and/or email for ExpenseBot configuration issues affecting Mosaic. Use conversations_search_messages(search_query: "ExpenseBot Mosaic") or conversations_search_messages(search_query: "Mosaic false approvals") or search_emails(query: "ExpenseBot Mosaic"). Agent finds Mosaic employees got out-of-policy expenses approved due to a malformed JSON config. Dmitri Volkov's audit determined the financial exposure at $885 in false approvals (Andre Washington's $1,200 standing desk exceeding $800 cap = $400 over limit, Andre Washington's $350 coworking membership in excluded category, Kenji Nakamura's $135 coworking day passes in excluded category). Samira reported the figure; Emeka acknowledged the $885 total exposure. Elena mandated a rollout pause.
OE 7: Search Slack, Airtable, and/or email for the flagged Mosaic employee receipt. Use conversations_search_messages(search_query: "receipt audit Mosaic") or conversations_search_messages(search_query: "Andre Washington receipt") or airtable_search_records(base_id: "...", table_name: "Stipend Transactions", field_name: "Employee Name", value: "Andre Washington"). Agent finds Andre Washington (Mosaic) submitted a byte-for-byte identical $89 receipt (same SHA-256 hash) as Kevin Tran (Vectral), both from vendor TechComfort Supply. Marcus put holds on pending payments. This is a separate issue from the ExpenseBot config bug in OE 6.
OE 8: Search email for whether Emeka already communicated with Tamara about the stipend/receipt issues. Use search_emails(query: "Emeka Tamara receipt") or search_emails(query: "verify receipt Mosaic"). Agent finds that Emeka contacted Tamara Byrd to verify Andre Washington's receipt, confirming he has already been in touch about this.
OE 9: Search Slack (#operations) and/or Linear for the compliance/documentation issue on a Mosaic equipment shipment. Use conversations_search_messages(search_query: "DOT Mosaic") or conversations_search_messages(search_query: "placarding certificate", filter_in_channel: #operations) or linear_list_issues(query: "DOT"). Agent finds Blessing Okafor never filed the DOT placarding certificate for Keiko Tanaka's laser cutter move (Columbus → Seattle). The 400-lb Epilog Fusion Pro industrial laser cutter required DOT hazmat placarding. Chloe created Linear tickets for each hazmat move's documentation status.
OE 10: Search email and/or Slack for whether the documentation gap was resolved. Use search_emails(query: "Greg Pallone certificate") or conversations_search_messages(search_query: "Swift certificate"). Agent finds Greg Pallone (Swift Relocations) confirmed the placarding was done and sent the signed certificate retroactively. The gap has been closed. Marcus also requested a 15-day DOT extension, which was granted.
OE 11: Look up contact details for Tamara Byrd (tamara.byrd@mosaicrobotics.com), Emeka Diallo, and Julian Brooks. Use contacts_search_contacts(query: "Tamara Byrd"), contacts_search_contacts(query: "Emeka Diallo"), contacts_search_contacts(query: "Julian Brooks").
OE 12: Log a CRM engagement note on the Mosaic account summarizing Tamara's support request and the current status of each issue (prototype damage, expense/stipend, DOT documentation). Use crm_create_engagement on the Mosaic company/deal found in OE 1.
OE 13: Update Airtable relocation records with complaint notes where missing — primarily Zara Okoye's record (prototype damage status, incident report, replacement shipping proposal) and Keiko Tanaka's record (DOT placarding gap and its resolution). Use airtable_update_records on records found in OE 2.
OE 14: Check Linear for existing tickets related to the prototype damage using linear_list_issues(query: "Mosaic prototype") or linear_list_issues(query: "prototype damage"). If none exists, create an ops follow-up ticket using linear_create_issue. If one exists, add a status comment using linear_create_comment. For the DOT documentation tickets Chloe created, add a status note confirming the resolution.
OE 15: Send Tamara Byrd an empathetic, professional email using send_email(to: "tamara.byrd@mosaicrobotics.com"). Acknowledge each issue she raised: prototype damage (incident report sent, replacement shipping proposed, Emeka and Julian engaged), expense/stipend issues (platform paused, Emeka in contact about receipt verification), equipment documentation (gap resolved). No commitments on resolutions or timelines.
OE 16: Send Emeka Diallo an email using send_email summarizing what Tamara asked about, what was communicated to her, and any items needing his follow-up as the account manager.
OE 17: Post a summary for Julian Brooks in #customer-support using conversations_add_message(channel: #customer-support) covering what Tamara asked, what was found on each issue, what was communicated to her, and any items needing further attention.

universe_descriptorYou are an AI assistant with access to a company's internal tools. Use the available tools to complete the entire task autonomously. No further user input will be given. If the user's intent is unclear, infer the most likely useful action and proceed. Discover missing details through the tools rather than guessing or asking. The current date is April 26, 2026. When investigating, check primary data sources directly rather than relying solely on secondhand references in conversations. Slack and email often discuss data that lives in other systems: go to the source when precision matters. About MoveOps Inc.: MoveOps is a corporate relocation management company. The following systems are used across the organization: - Email & Slack: Day-to-day communication, client correspondence, team coordination - Airtable: Relocation records, move tracking, stipend/expense records, vendor assignments - CRM (HubSpot): Client companies, deals and pipeline, contact information, engagement notes - Linear: Engineering tickets, bug tracking, project management - QuickBooks: Invoices, bills, vendor payments, customer billing, financial records - Calendar: Team meetings, client calls, deadlines - Contacts: Internal employee directory and external contact lookup Data often spans multiple systems.
universe_idmcp-advanced-3-1-moveops-v3
